assistant_intro: Hi there! Welcome. I'm really looking forward to our chat today about the Backend Developer role. We'll dive into some technical areas, and later on, we might touch on a few behavioral aspects, but for now, let's focus on your technical journey.

questions.question: To kick things off, could you walk me through some of the backend projects you've worked on, perhaps highlighting one where you felt you made a significant impact on its maintainability or performance? What technologies (languages, frameworks, databases, tools) did you primarily use in that context, and why did you choose them?
expected_points: Specific programming languages used (e.g., Python, Node.js)
Backend frameworks utilized (e.g., FastAPI, Django, Express.js)
Database systems and types (e.g., PostgreSQL, MongoDB)
Other relevant tools or services (e.g., Docker, Git, cloud platforms)
Brief context of the project and the specific challenge/impact related to maintainability/performance
evaluation_focus: Clarity and coherence in describing their experience
Breadth of backend technologies they've actively used
Ability to articulate reasoning behind technology choices
Relevance of their experience to real-world backend development

question_focus: REST API design principles and best practices

interview_variation.freshness_rule: Make this Backend Developer interview feel fresh for seed 68e160 and avoid reusing stock boilerplate wording.

question_outline.question: To kick things off, could you walk me through some of the backend projects you've worked on, perhaps highlighting one where you felt you made a significant impact on its maintainability or performance? What technologies (languages, frameworks, databases, tools) did you primarily use in that context, and why did you choose them?
